- [ ] 1. **Build "MeetingScribe" Chrome Extension (production-quality, Web Store-ready)** — A comprehensive Chrome Extension with 11 phases: (1) Scaffold + build system (Vite + crxjs + React 18 + TS + Tailwind + Tiptap v2); (2) Core types + routing engine with Vitest tests; (3) Auth layer (chrome.identity for Google, launchWebAuthFlow + PKCE for Microsoft, Dropbox OAuth2); (4) Calendar API integrations (Google Calendar v3, Microsoft Graph) + dial-in stripping utility + note template builder; (5) Content scripts for Google Calendar and Outlook 365 with MutationObserver + injected "Take Notes" button; (6) Background service worker with chrome.alarms for auto-open polling and notifications; (7) Notes window React app with Tiptap rich-text editor, meeting header, save button with routing engine integration, auto-save drafts; (8) Upload modules for Google Drive, Dropbox, OneDrive; (9) Options page React app with OAuth connect/disconnect, routing rules CRUD with folder picker, general settings; (10) Comprehensive README covering Google Cloud Console, Azure App Registration, Dropbox app setup; (11) Chrome Web Store preparation (package script, privacy policy, permissions justification, store assets). Tech: Manifest V3, strict TypeScript (no `any` except where required), real error handling throughout, ESLint + Prettier, Vitest. Work phase-by-phase pausing after each for user confirmation. Interactive walkthrough of all external console steps (Google Cloud, Azure Portal, Chrome extensions page) before continuing.
  **Scoping decisions captured in PROJECT_DECISIONS.md (2026-04-09):** Q1=rename folder to MeetingScribe (user action), Q2=npm, Q3=turndown only, Q4=**Tailwind v4** (spec overridden), Q5=OAuth walk-throughs upfront (before scaffolding), Q6=Vitest installed in Phase 1. **Session paused** so user can rename folder `ManualMeetingMinutes` → `MeetingScribe`. Next session resumes with Phase 0 (OAuth consoles upfront, partial — full completion deferred until first unpacked Chrome build exists), then Phase 1 scaffolding.

  - [X] 1b. Phase 2 — Core TypeScript types (src/types/global.d.ts) + routing engine (src/routing/engine.ts) + Vitest tests
  - [X] 1c. Phase 3 — Auth layer **(revised for multi-account, Q7 decision 2026-04-09)**. All three providers use `chrome.identity.launchWebAuthFlow()` + PKCE + refresh tokens. Token store shape: `{ google: ConnectedAccount[], microsoft: ConnectedAccount[], dropbox: ConnectedAccount[] }` in `chrome.storage.local`. Each `ConnectedAccount` = `{ id, email, displayName, accessToken, refreshToken, expiresAt, scopes, addedAt }`. Refresh flow handles expiry. Per-account disconnect (revokes refresh token, removes from store).
  - [X] 1d. Phase 4 — Calendar API integrations **(revised for multi-account)**. Iterate over ALL connected Google + Microsoft accounts; fetch events from each (`GET /calendars/primary/events` for Google, `GET /me/events` for Microsoft); merge into unified timeline sorted by `start`. Strip-dialin util + buildNoteTemplate unchanged. UX must hide "source: X" labels when only one calendar source is connected.
  - [X] 1e. Phase 5 — Content scripts (gcal.ts + outlook.ts with MutationObserver, injected "Take Notes" button, fallback selectors)
  - [X] 1f. Phase 6 — Background service worker (message handler for openNotes, chrome.alarms polling every 10min, per-event scheduled alarms, chrome.notifications)
  - [X] 1g. Phase 7 — Notes window React app **(revised for multi-account)**. Tiptap editor + toolbar + MeetingHeader. SaveButton opens a **destination picker** listing all connected accounts across all providers (e.g. "Dropbox — danspiegel@danspiegelllc.com", "Google Drive — danspiegel@gmail.com", "OneDrive — dan.spiegel@blacknile.ai"), pre-selected by matching routing rule, user can override before clicking Save. Auto-save drafts + word count unchanged.
  - [X] 1h. Phase 8 — Upload modules **(revised for multi-account)**. Each upload function takes an explicit `ConnectedAccount` argument (not a global token). Google Drive v3 multipart, Dropbox `/files/upload`, Microsoft Graph `/me/drive` PUT. Returns `{ provider, accountId, fileUrl, fileId }`.
  - [X] 1i. Phase 9 — Options page React app **(revised for multi-account)**. `ConnectedAccounts` section = list per provider with "Add account" button and per-row disconnect. `RoutingRules` references `{ provider, accountId, folderPath }` instead of just provider; folder picker modal scoped to the selected account. Drag reorder + GeneralSettings unchanged.
